title: NTC Temperature Sensor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor has a short circuit, preventing accurate water temperature measurement.
fullDescription: Error F01 on Miele dishwashers indicates the NTC temperature sensor in the wash system has a short circuit. The control board reads near-zero resistance from the sensor and cannot regulate water heating. The dishwasher will stop the cycle to prevent uncontrolled heating.

causes.cause: NTC sensor element has internally shorted
Sensor wiring is pinched or shorted to chassis
Water intrusion into the sensor connector
Control board sensor input circuit failure

symptoms: F01 error on display or LED blink code
Dishes come out dirty and cold
Error appears early in the wash program

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher, unplug it for 5 minutes, then restart. If F01 returns, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower kick plate and inner door panel if needed. The NTC sensor is usually mounted in the sump area or on the circulation pump housing.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor wiring. Measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (20C), expect approximately 10-15 kOhms. Near-zero reading confirms short circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty sensor from its housing. Install the replacement sensor, ensuring the seal is intact. Reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test normal, the control board input circuit may be faulty and requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F01 NTC temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor in a dishwasher?
faq.a: An NTC sensor measures water temperature. Its resistance decreases as temperature rises, allowing the control board to precisely monitor and regulate water heating.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with F01?
faq.a: No. The dishwasher will not heat water or complete wash cycles with F01 active. Dishes will not be properly cleaned or sanitized.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor expensive?
faq.a: NTC sensors for Miele dishwashers are relatively inexpensive at 15-35 dollars. The repair is straightforward for someone with basic appliance repair skills.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor circuit is open, providing no signal to the control board.
fullDescription: Error F02 means the control board reads infinite resistance from the NTC sensor, indicating a broken connection. The sensor, its wiring, or its connector has an open circuit, preventing temperature measurement.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has failed open circuit
Sensor connector has come loose
Wire in the sensor circuit is broken
Corrosion on connector pins

symptoms: Cold water wash results in dirty dishes

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the dishwasher for 5 minutes. Reconnect and test. A loose connector may have been temporarily fixed by vibration.

fixSteps.title: Check connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the NTC sensor. Ensure the wire connector is firmly plugged in. Push until it clicks or is fully seated.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure with a multimeter. Expect 10-15 kOhms at room temperature. OL (infinite) reading confirms open circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor or repair wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the sensor if open. If the sensor is good, trace and repair the broken wire using crimp connectors or solder.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring are intact, the control board may have a damaged input. Call a Miele technician.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F02 NTC sensor open circuit error

faq.q: What is the difference between F01 and F02?
faq.a: F01 is a short circuit (zero resistance) while F02 is an open circuit (infinite resistance). Both indicate NTC sensor failure but from opposite fault modes.

faq.q: Can a loose wire cause F02?
faq.a: Yes. A loose or corroded connector is one of the most common causes. Always check connections before replacing the sensor.

faq.q: How long does it take to replace the NTC sensor?
faq.a: Typically 20-30 minutes for someone comfortable with basic dishwasher disassembly. The sensor itself is a quick swap once accessed.

title: Drain Pump Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot drain water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error F11 means the drain pump could not empty the sump within the expected timeframe. Water remains in the bottom of the dishwasher. This is one of the most common Miele dishwasher errors, usually caused by food debris blocking the drain path.

causes.cause: Drain filter and sump are clogged with food debris
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller is jammed
Drain pump motor has failed
Kitchen sink drain or garbage disposal is blocked

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door. Remove the lower rack. Twist and lift out the drain filter assembly from the sump. Rinse under running water and scrub away food debris with a brush.

fixSteps.title: Check the sump area
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, check the sump area for large food particles, broken glass, or other debris. Remove anything blocking the drain opening.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out and check the drain hose for kinks or blockages. Disconnect from the sink drain or garbage disposal and check for clogs at the connection point.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter, sump, and hose are clear, listen for the drain pump running during a drain cycle. If silent, access the pump and test the motor winding resistance (typically 10-30 ohms).

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the filter and checking the hose does not resolve F11, and the pump motor is silent, a technician can replace the drain pump.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F11 drain error filter cleaning fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the Miele dishwasher filter?
faq.a: Miele recommends cleaning the drain filter after every few cycles or at least once a week for optimal performance and to prevent F11 errors.

faq.q: Why is there water in the bottom of my Miele dishwasher?
faq.a: Standing water after a cycle indicates a drain problem. The drain filter is the most likely culprit. Remove it and clean thoroughly.

faq.q: Can a garbage disposal cause F11?
faq.a: Yes. If your dishwasher drains through a garbage disposal, ensure the disposal knockout plug was removed during installation and the disposal is not clogged.

title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not receiving enough water during the fill cycle.
fullDescription: Error F12 indicates the dishwasher did not detect sufficient water intake within the allowed time. The water level sensor or flow meter did not register adequate flow, preventing the wash cycle from proceeding.

causes.cause: Water supply tap is closed or partially closed
Inlet hose is kinked or blocked
Inlet valve filter screen is clogged
Water inlet valve has failed

symptoms: No water or very little water entering the dishwasher
Wash arms not spinning due to insufficient water
Cycle does not progress past the fill phase
Humming from inlet valve but no water flow

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply tap (usually under the kitchen sink) is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the inlet hose for kinks. Turn off the supply, disconnect the hose from the dishwasher, and check flow by holding it over a bucket and briefly turning on the tap.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the inlet hose from the dishwasher valve. Extract the small mesh filter screen and clean it under running water. Remove any sediment.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If water supply and filters are fine, test the inlet valve solenoid resistance (typically 2-5 kOhms). A faulty valve needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the valve and supply are fine but water still does not enter, a technician should check the control board and flow meter.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F12 water inlet fill error fix

faq.q: Where is the water supply tap for my dishwasher?
faq.a: The water supply valve is usually located under the kitchen sink. It may be a dedicated valve for the dishwasher line. Ensure it is fully open.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause F12?
faq.a: Yes. Miele dishwashers require adequate water pressure to fill properly. If other fixtures also have low pressure, the problem is with your water supply.

faq.q: How do I clean the inlet filter screen?
faq.a: Turn off the water supply, disconnect the inlet hose from the dishwasher, and use pliers to gently extract the small mesh filter from inside the valve inlet. Rinse under running water.

title: Water Intake Timeout
shortDescription: Water intake has taken too long to reach the required level.
fullDescription: Error F13 is similar to F12 but specifically indicates a timeout condition. Water is entering the machine but too slowly to reach the required level within the time limit. This often points to partially restricted flow rather than a complete blockage.

causes.cause: Partially clogged inlet filter reducing flow rate
Water supply pressure is marginal
Inlet valve partially stuck, limiting flow
Flow meter not accurately counting water volume

symptoms: F13 error after a long fill attempt
Dishwasher takes much longer than normal to fill
Other water fixtures show reduced flow

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Open the kitchen faucet to check water pressure. If it is weak, F13 may be caused by low supply pressure. Check if other fixtures are using water simultaneously.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the inlet hose filter screen. Even partial clogging can slow flow enough to cause a timeout.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet hose for restrictions
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the full length of the inlet hose for partial kinks, tight bends, or compression. Replace if the hose is old and internally deteriorated.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve flow rate
fixSteps.instruction: With the hose disconnected at the dishwasher end, time how long it takes to fill a known container. Compare to expected flow rate (typically 2-4 liters per minute minimum).

whenToCallPro: If cleaning filters and checking supply does not resolve the slow fill, a plumber may need to check your water supply pressure.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F13 slow water fill timeout error

faq.q: Is F13 the same as F12?
faq.a: Similar but not identical. F12 typically means no water at all, while F13 means water is entering but too slowly. Both relate to water intake issues.

faq.q: Can running other appliances cause F13?
faq.a: Yes. If a washing machine or shower is running simultaneously, water pressure to the dishwasher may drop below the required minimum.

faq.q: How do I check my water pressure?
faq.a: You can buy a simple water pressure gauge that screws onto a faucet. Normal household pressure is 40-60 PSI. Below 20 PSI may cause fill issues.

title: Circulation Pump Pressure Error
shortDescription: The circulation pump is not generating sufficient pressure for the wash spray arms.
fullDescription: Error F14 indicates the circulation pump is running but not producing enough pressure to properly operate the spray arms. The pressure or flow sensor detects insufficient flow through the wash system.

causes.cause: Wash arms are clogged with food debris or limescale
Circulation pump impeller is worn or damaged
Foreign object blocking the pump inlet
Circulation pump motor is failing

symptoms: Wash arms not spinning or spinning slowly
Low water spray visible through door window

fixSteps.title: Clean the spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove upper and lower spray arms. Clear each nozzle hole with a toothpick or thin wire. Rinse under running water. Reattach and ensure they spin freely.

fixSteps.title: Check the sump for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the drain filter and inspect the sump area. Look for broken glass, large food particles, or other objects that could restrict water flow to the circulation pump.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run an empty hot cycle with Miele dishwasher cleaner or citric acid to dissolve limescale buildup in the spray arms and pump.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not help, access the circulation pump from underneath the dishwasher. Check the impeller for damage or wear. A worn impeller cannot generate adequate pressure.

whenToCallPro: Circulation pump replacement involves accessing the pump from underneath the dishwasher and handling water connections. Professional service ensures no leaks.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F14 circulation pump spray arm error

faq.q: How do I remove Miele dishwasher spray arms?
faq.a: The lower spray arm usually pulls straight off. The upper spray arm may unscrew counterclockwise. Some models have a center cap that needs to be removed first.

faq.q: Can limescale cause F14?
faq.a: Yes. Limescale buildup in the spray arm nozzles reduces flow and pressure. Regular use of rinse aid and periodic descaling prevents this.

faq.q: What does the circulation pump do?
faq.a: The circulation pump pressurizes water and forces it through the spray arms to wash dishes. It is the main pump that operates during the wash and rinse phases.

title: Hot Water Intake Error
shortDescription: Hot water detected during a phase that requires cold water, or vice versa.
fullDescription: Error F15 signals that the incoming water temperature does not match what the control system expects. This can occur if the dishwasher is connected to a hot water supply when it expects cold, or if the NTC sensor is misreading the inlet water temperature.

causes.cause: Dishwasher connected to hot water instead of cold
NTC sensor giving inaccurate readings
Inlet valve mixing hot and cold incorrectly
Hot water supply temperature exceeds dishwasher maximum

symptoms: Error at the start of the fill cycle
Steam visible when door is opened
Water feels very hot during what should be a pre-rinse
Dishes may show heat damage on plastics

fixSteps.title: Check water connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the dishwasher is connected to the correct water supply. Most Miele dishwashers should be connected to cold water only unless specified otherwise in the installation manual.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet water temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the inlet hose and run water into a container. Test with a thermometer. The temperature should match your cold water supply (typically below 25C).

fixSteps.title: Check NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the water connection is correct, test the NTC sensor to ensure it is reading accurately. Compare its resistance to the temperature chart in the service manual.

fixSteps.title: Correct the water connection
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to hot water incorrectly, reconnect to the cold water supply. Ensure the inlet hose connects to the cold water valve under the sink.

whenToCallPro: If the water connection is correct and the NTC sensor tests fine, a professional should check the inlet valve and control board.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F15 hot water intake error fix

faq.q: Should my Miele dishwasher use hot or cold water?
faq.a: Most Miele dishwashers are designed for cold water connection. Some models support hot water up to 60C. Check your installation manual for your specific model.

faq.q: Can hot water damage my dishwasher?
faq.a: Water above 60C can damage plastic components and seals. Always follow Miele's water connection guidelines for your model.

faq.q: Why does F15 matter if the water will be heated anyway?
faq.a: The dishwasher's programs are designed to heat water from a known starting temperature. Starting with hot water disrupts temperature control and can damage sensitive items.

title: Heating Timeout
shortDescription: The water did not reach the target temperature within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error F18 means the dishwasher's heating system could not raise water temperature to the programmed level within the time limit. The heating element, NTC sensor, or water circulation may be at fault.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out
Limescale buildup on heating element
NTC sensor reading inaccurately
Control board not powering the heater

symptoms: Dishes not properly cleaned or dried
Cycle takes much longer before aborting
Works on quick wash but fails on intensive programs

fixSteps.title: Run a descaling cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run an empty cycle with Miele descaling agent or citric acid. Limescale on the heater dramatically reduces heating efficiency.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heating element from underneath the dishwasher. Disconnect and measure resistance (typically 15-30 ohms). Also check insulation to ground (should be over 1 MOhm).

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test NTC resistance at room temperature (10-15 kOhms). An inaccurate sensor can prevent proper heating control.

fixSteps.title: Replace heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater tests open or high resistance, replace it. This typically involves removing the sump assembly to access the heater.

whenToCallPro: If the heater and sensor test fine, the control board may not be sending power to the heating circuit. A technician should test the relay.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F18 heating timeout error fix

faq.q: How does the dishwasher heat water?
faq.a: Miele dishwashers use a flow-through heater or an element in the sump to heat water. The circulation pump moves water past the heater during the heating phase.

faq.q: Can I use vinegar instead of Miele descaler?
faq.a: White vinegar or citric acid can be used as alternatives to Miele's branded descaler. However, Miele recommends their own products for optimal results.

faq.q: How often should I descale my dishwasher?
faq.a: Every 3-6 months depending on your water hardness. Hard water areas may need monthly descaling.

title: Flow Meter Error
shortDescription: The water flow meter is not detecting water flow properly.
fullDescription: Error F19 indicates the flow meter (water turbine sensor) is not registering water flow despite the inlet valve being open. The flow meter counts water volume entering the machine.

causes.cause: Flow meter is clogged with scale deposits
Flow meter turbine is seized
Flow meter electrical sensor has failed
Wiring to flow meter is damaged

symptoms: Water enters but cycle does not advance

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filters
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the hose filter screens to ensure good water flow to the flow meter.

fixSteps.title: Access the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: The flow meter is in the inlet valve assembly. Remove the kickplate and locate it on the inlet water path.

fixSteps.title: Clean the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the flow meter and flush with water and vinegar to dissolve scale. Ensure the turbine spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Replace flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not help, replace the flow meter unit. Disconnect electrical and water connections, swap, and test.

whenToCallPro: If a new flow meter does not resolve F19, the control board flow meter input may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F19 flow meter error fix

faq.q: What does a flow meter do?
faq.a: The flow meter counts the volume of water entering the dishwasher using a spinning turbine and magnetic sensor. This ensures the correct amount of water is used per cycle.

faq.q: Can hard water cause F19?
faq.a: Yes. Mineral scale deposits can clog the flow meter turbine, preventing it from spinning and counting water volume accurately.

faq.q: Do all Miele dishwashers have a flow meter?
faq.a: Most newer Miele dishwashers (G5000 and G7000 series) include a flow meter for precise water management. Some older models rely on pressure switches.

title: Heating Relay Fault
shortDescription: The heating element relay on the control board has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error F24 indicates the control board's heating relay is not switching correctly. The relay may be stuck open (heater will not turn on) or stuck closed (heater stays on). This is a control board fault requiring electronic repair.

causes.cause: Heating relay contacts are welded shut
Relay coil has failed
Heating element ground fault causing relay damage
Power surge damaged the relay circuit

symptoms: Water does not heat or overheats

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. If F24 was caused by a transient event, it may clear.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Check the heater for ground faults (insulation to chassis should be over 1 MOhm). A ground fault can damage the relay.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the control board cover and visually inspect the heating relay for burn marks or swelling.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the relay is damaged, replace the control board. Transfer settings to the new board.

whenToCallPro: F24 requires control board replacement which should be done by a Miele-certified technician for proper configuration.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F24 heating relay control board error

faq.q: Can I repair the relay without replacing the whole board?
faq.a: Technically possible with SMD soldering skills, but Miele does not support component-level repairs. Board replacement is the recommended approach.

faq.q: Is F24 a fire hazard?
faq.a: A relay stuck closed can cause continuous heating. The dishwasher has safety thermal cutouts, but F24 should be repaired promptly.

faq.q: How much does a Miele dishwasher control board cost?
faq.a: Expect to pay 200-450 for the board and 100-200 for professional installation.

title: Drying System Error
shortDescription: The drying system is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error F25 relates to the drying system. On Miele dishwashers with AutoOpen or condensation drying, this error indicates the drying mechanism or its sensors have failed. The door may not auto-open or the drying fan may not operate.

causes.cause: Drying fan motor has failed
AutoOpen door mechanism is jammed
Drying sensor is faulty
Wiring to drying components is damaged

symptoms: Dishes remain wet after drying phase
Door does not auto-open on models with AutoOpen
Condensation on the inside of the door

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is blocking the door from opening (nearby cabinet, items on top). Check the AutoOpen mechanism for stuck or jammed parts.

fixSteps.title: Test the drying fan
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the fan during the drying phase. If silent, access the fan motor and check wiring connections.

fixSteps.title: Inspect AutoOpen mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Check the actuator that pushes the door open. Ensure it moves freely and the spring mechanism is intact.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the fan motor, AutoOpen actuator, or sensor as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the AutoOpen mechanism requires recalibration or the control board is not sending signals to the drying system, professional service is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F25 drying AutoOpen error fix

faq.q: What is AutoOpen drying?
faq.a: AutoOpen is Miele's feature where the dishwasher door automatically opens slightly at the end of the cycle to allow steam to escape and dishes to dry naturally.

faq.q: Can I dry dishes manually if AutoOpen is broken?
faq.a: Yes. Simply open the door yourself after the cycle finishes. The dishes will still dry, just not as efficiently as with the automatic system.

faq.q: Is F25 urgent to fix?
faq.a: F25 is not critical for washing. Your dishwasher will still clean dishes. However, drying performance will be reduced until the issue is fixed.

title: Diverter Valve Error
shortDescription: The water diverter valve is not switching between spray arm zones correctly.
fullDescription: Error F26 means the diverter valve that routes water between the upper and lower spray arms is stuck or malfunctioning. This valve alternates water flow between washing zones for optimal cleaning performance.

causes.cause: Diverter valve is jammed with debris
Diverter valve motor has failed
Diverter valve position sensor is faulty
Wiring to diverter valve is damaged

symptoms: Only upper or lower spray arm works
Dishes in one rack are clean while the other rack is dirty
Clicking or grinding noise from diverter area

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug for 5 minutes. Restart and observe if both spray arms receive water.

fixSteps.title: Check for debris
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the sump filter and check around the diverter valve area for food debris or broken glass that could jam the valve.

fixSteps.title: Access the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: The diverter valve is located in the sump area. Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly to access it. Check if the valve moves freely.

fixSteps.title: Replace the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve is damaged or the motor is faulty, replace the diverter valve assembly. Disconnect wiring, remove mounting screws, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the valve appears to work mechanically but the error persists, the position sensor or control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F26 diverter valve error fix

faq.q: What is a diverter valve in a dishwasher?
faq.a: The diverter valve alternates water flow between the upper and lower spray arms (and sometimes a ceiling spray arm) to maximize cleaning coverage while minimizing water usage.

faq.q: How do I know which spray arm is not working?
faq.a: Place a small piece of paper on the top and bottom racks. Run a quick cycle. If one piece is wet and the other dry, the corresponding spray arm zone is not receiving water.

faq.q: Can debris jam the diverter valve?
faq.a: Yes. Food particles, broken glass, or utensil pieces can enter the sump and jam the valve. Always scrape dishes before loading and keep the filter clean.

title: Door Switch Error
shortDescription: The door switch is not registering the door as properly closed.
fullDescription: Error F32 indicates the dishwasher door switch does not detect the door in the closed position. The dishwasher requires a confirmed closed door before starting any cycle.

causes.cause: Door latch is not fully engaging
Door switch has failed
Door hinges are loose causing misalignment
Wiring to door switch is disconnected

symptoms: Dishwasher will not start any program
Door appears closed but display shows it as open
No latch click when closing the door

fixSteps.title: Close the door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Push the door closed with a firm push until you hear or feel the latch click. Check for dishes or the rack preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door slowly, watching how the latch engages the strike. If misaligned, adjust the door hinges or strike plate.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the door switch behind the inner door panel. Test for continuity when the latch is engaged. The switch should close when the door latches.

fixSteps.title: Replace door switch or latch
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component. The door latch or switch can usually be replaced by removing the inner door panel and swapping the part.

whenToCallPro: If the latch and switch are fine but F32 persists, the control board door input may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F32 door switch latch error fix

faq.q: Why won't my Miele dishwasher door latch?
faq.a: Check that no dishes, utensils, or the upper rack are blocking the door from closing completely. Also check that the door hinges are not loose.

faq.q: Can I force the door closed?
faq.a: Never force the dishwasher door. This can damage the latch mechanism. If it does not close easily, investigate the obstruction or alignment issue.

faq.q: How do I access the door switch?
faq.a: Remove the inner door panel screws (usually Torx) to access the switch mounted behind the latch mechanism on the door frame.

title: Door Lock Failure
shortDescription: The electronic door lock is not engaging or releasing properly.
fullDescription: Error F33 means the electronic door lock mechanism is malfunctioning. The lock secures the door during operation to prevent accidental opening. The lock solenoid or its feedback circuit has failed.

causes.cause: Door lock solenoid has failed
Lock mechanism is mechanically stuck
Lock feedback switch is not confirming engagement
Control board lock circuit failure

symptoms: Door locks but error still appears
Door will not lock when cycle starts
Clicking from lock area without engagement

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. The lock should release when power is removed. Replug and test.

fixSteps.title: Inspect lock mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Open the inner door panel to access the lock. Check for debris or corrosion preventing smooth operation.

fixSteps.title: Test lock solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the solenoid coil resistance (typically 100-500 ohms). Open circuit means the solenoid has failed.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screws, disconnect wiring, and install the new lock. Test operation before reassembling.

whenToCallPro: If the door remains stuck locked and you cannot access the lock mechanism, a technician can safely release it.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F33 door lock error fix

faq.q: How do I unlock a stuck Miele dishwasher door?
faq.a: Unplug the dishwasher for 5-10 minutes. Most Miele models release the door lock when power is removed. If it remains locked, contact Miele service.

faq.q: Is the door lock a safety feature?
faq.a: Yes. The lock prevents the door from being opened during a cycle when hot water and steam are present. Never try to bypass the lock.

faq.q: Can I start the dishwasher without the lock working?
faq.a: No. The dishwasher requires confirmation that the door is locked before it will run any cycle.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between the main control board and another module.
fullDescription: Error F34 in Miele dishwashers indicates a data communication failure between the main control board and one of the peripheral modules such as the display board or sensor interface module. The internal data bus is disrupted.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable between boards is loose or damaged
Control board has a communication bus failure
Display/interface board has failed
Moisture on the control board

fixSteps.title: Hard reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 15 minutes. This allows all capacitors to discharge and can clear communication glitches.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the door panel to access the boards. Unplug and reseat all ribbon cables and connectors between the main board and display board.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the control board area for moisture or condensation. Dry any moisture found and check that door seals are preventing steam entry.

fixSteps.title: Replace affected board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating cables does not work, the main board or display board may need replacement. A technician can determine which.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not resolve F34, professional diagnosis with Miele tools is needed to identify the failed module.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F34 communication error control board

faq.q: Can steam damage the control board?
faq.a: Yes. If door seals are worn, steam can enter the control board housing and cause corrosion or short circuits. Inspect and replace worn seals.

faq.q: Is F34 always a board failure?
faq.a: No. Often it is just a loose cable connection. Always try reseating connections before assuming a board has failed.

faq.q: Can I replace a Miele dishwasher control board myself?
faq.a: Physically swapping the board is straightforward. However, some boards need programming or configuration for your specific model, which may require Miele service software.

title: Program Selector Error
shortDescription: The program selector or user interface is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error F35 means the control board cannot read the program selection from the user interface. The selector switch, touch panel, or interface board has a fault.

causes.cause: Touch panel or selector switch is faulty
Interface board encoder has worn out
Cable between interface and main board is loose
Moisture behind the control panel

symptoms: Wrong program selected when button pressed

fixSteps.title: Clean the control panel
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the control panel area with a dry cloth. Moisture or residue on touch-sensitive panels can cause false inputs.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart. This resets the interface board and can clear temporary faults.

fixSteps.title: Reseat interface cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door panel and reseat the cable connecting the interface board to the main control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace interface board
fixSteps.instruction: If the fault persists, replace the interface/display board. Disconnect cables, swap the board, and test.

whenToCallPro: If the interface board replacement is needed, ensure compatibility with your model or have a technician perform the repair.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F35 program selector interface error

faq.q: Why don't my dishwasher buttons respond?
faq.a: Moisture, cleaning residue, or a faulty touch panel can prevent button response. Clean the panel and try a power reset first.

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher if only some buttons work?
faq.a: Temporarily, you may be able to select a working program. However, the interface should be repaired for full functionality.

faq.q: Do Miele dishwashers have touch screens?
faq.a: Newer Miele models have touch-sensitive control panels. Older models use physical buttons and rotary dials. F35 can affect either type.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The incoming power supply voltage is outside acceptable limits.
fullDescription: Error F36 means the control board has detected that the incoming AC voltage is too high or too low for safe operation. This protects the dishwasher electronics from damage due to brownouts or overvoltage conditions.

causes.cause: Household voltage is too low (brownout)
Household voltage is too high
Loose or corroded power connection
Control board voltage sensing circuit is faulty

symptoms: Other appliances on same circuit also affected
Lights in the house dim when dishwasher attempts to run

fixSteps.title: Check household voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Use a voltage tester at the dishwasher outlet. Normal voltage should be 220-240V (Europe) or 110-120V (US). Readings below or above this range cause F36.

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dishwasher is on a dedicated circuit and not sharing with other high-draw appliances. Check the circuit breaker for a good connection.

fixSteps.title: Check power cord and connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the power cord for damage. Check the junction box connection under the dishwasher for loose or corroded wires.

fixSteps.title: Contact an electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If household voltage is consistently out of range, an electrician should check your home's electrical system, transformer, and panel.

whenToCallPro: If voltage at the outlet is out of range, call an electrician. If voltage is normal but F36 persists, a Miele technician should check the voltage sensing circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F36 power voltage error fix

faq.q: What voltage does a Miele dishwasher need?
faq.a: In North America, 120V/60Hz. In Europe, 220-240V/50Hz. Check your model's rating plate for the exact specification.

faq.q: Can a voltage stabilizer help?
faq.a: If your area has frequent voltage fluctuations, a voltage stabilizer or UPS can protect the dishwasher and other sensitive electronics.

faq.q: Why does voltage drop during high-demand periods?
faq.a: When many appliances run simultaneously in your home or neighborhood, voltage can drop (brownout). This is more common in older electrical systems.

title: EEPROM Data Error
shortDescription: The EEPROM memory on the control board has corrupted data.
fullDescription: Error F37 indicates the non-volatile memory (EEPROM) on the control board has corrupted data. Configuration settings, program parameters, or calibration data cannot be read correctly. This typically requires control board reprogramming or replacement.

causes.cause: Power surge corrupted EEPROM data
EEPROM chip has worn out after many write cycles
Control board component failure affecting memory bus
Interrupted firmware update

symptoms: F37 error on display immediately on power-up
Error appeared after a power outage

fixSteps.title: Extended power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes to allow full discharge. This may allow the board to reinitialize correctly.

fixSteps.title: Attempt factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Some models support a factory reset via button combination. Consult the service manual for your model's reset procedure.

fixSteps.title: Check for voltage issues
fixSteps.instruction: Verify stable power supply at the outlet to prevent future corruption.

fixSteps.title: Professional reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: A Miele technician can reprogram the EEPROM using service software. If the chip is physically damaged, the control board must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: F37 almost always requires professional service with Miele diagnostic tools to reprogram or replace the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F37 EEPROM memory error fix

faq.q: What is EEPROM?
faq.a: EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) is a chip on the control board that stores the dishwasher's configuration, program parameters, and calibration data.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F37?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges and sudden outages can corrupt EEPROM data. Using a surge protector helps prevent this.

faq.q: Will I lose my custom settings after repair?
faq.a: If the EEPROM is reprogrammed, settings will be restored to factory defaults. You will need to reconfigure your preferred settings.

title: Control Board General Failure
shortDescription: The main control board has a general hardware failure.
fullDescription: Error F40 is a general control board failure code that does not point to a specific subsystem. The board's self-diagnostic has detected an internal fault that prevents normal operation. This typically requires board replacement.

causes.cause: Control board component failure
Power surge damaged the control board
Age-related capacitor failure on the board
Moisture damage to the control board

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 15 minutes. In some cases a transient fault can cause F40.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Open the board housing and look for visible damage: burnt components, swollen capacitors, corrosion, or water damage.

fixSteps.title: Check all connections
fixSteps.instruction: Reseat all connectors on the control board. A poor connection can sometimes mimic a board failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If F40 persists, replace the main control board. Ensure the replacement is correct for your model and firmware version.

whenToCallPro: F40 is a board failure that requires professional replacement and possibly firmware programming.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F40 control board failure replacement

faq.q: Is F40 repairable?
faq.a: F40 requires control board replacement. While the board swap is straightforward, some models need professional programming of the new board.

faq.q: How long do Miele control boards last?
faq.a: Miele control boards typically last 8-15 years. Boards can fail prematurely from power surges, moisture, or manufacturing defects.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the board on an old dishwasher?
faq.a: If the dishwasher is over 10 years old and the board costs 300+, compare the total repair cost to a new dishwasher. Miele machines are built to last 20 years, so a board replacement can still be worthwhile.

title: Inverter Module Error
shortDescription: The circulation pump inverter module has detected a fault.
fullDescription: Error F42 indicates the inverter module that controls the circulation pump motor has a fault. The inverter converts power to drive the pump at variable speeds for optimal wash performance.

causes.cause: Inverter module has overheated
Inverter module component failure
Circulation pump motor has a winding fault
Power supply to inverter is unstable

symptoms: Wash pump does not start or runs erratically
Error during the main wash phase
Pump makes unusual noises before error

fixSteps.title: Let dishwasher cool
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug and let cool for 30 minutes. Overheating inverters may recover after cooling.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is blocking airflow around the dishwasher. The inverter needs ventilation to prevent overheating.

fixSteps.title: Test pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pump and measure winding resistance. Equal readings between phases and high insulation to ground indicate a good motor.

fixSteps.title: Replace inverter or pump
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component. The inverter may be integral to the pump assembly on some models, requiring a complete pump replacement.

whenToCallPro: Inverter and pump replacement involves water connections under the dishwasher. Professional service prevents leaks and ensures proper operation.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F42 inverter circulation pump error

faq.q: What does the inverter do in a dishwasher?
faq.a: The inverter controls the speed of the circulation pump motor, allowing the dishwasher to adjust water pressure for different wash phases (gentle, normal, intensive).

faq.q: Can overheating cause F42?
faq.a: Yes. If the dishwasher is enclosed without adequate ventilation, the inverter can overheat and trigger F42. Ensure proper airflow around the unit.

faq.q: Is the inverter separate from the pump?
faq.a: On some Miele models, the inverter is a separate module. On newer models, it may be integrated into the pump assembly, requiring a complete pump replacement.

title: Communication Bus Error
shortDescription: Data bus communication between internal components has failed.
fullDescription: Error F47 signals a failure on the internal communication bus connecting the dishwasher's electronic modules. Data cannot be exchanged between the processor and peripheral controllers.

causes.cause: Loose internal wiring connector
Control board bus driver failure
Electromagnetic interference
Damaged wiring harness

symptoms: Multiple error codes appear in sequence

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 15 minutes. Bus errors can be transient.

fixSteps.title: Reseat all connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher panels and reseat every internal connector. Focus on the main board and any sub-boards.

fixSteps.title: Check for interference
fixSteps.instruction: Move the dishwasher to a different circuit if possible. Ensure no large motors or compressors share the same outlet.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: A Miele technician with diagnostic software can identify the specific module causing the bus error.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connectors does not fix F47, professional diagnosis is needed to identify the failed module.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F47 communication bus error

faq.q: What is a communication bus?
faq.a: A communication bus is the wiring and protocol that allows electronic modules inside the dishwasher to exchange data. It is like a small internal network.

faq.q: Can EMI cause F47?
faq.a: Yes. Strong electromagnetic interference from nearby appliances can disrupt the data bus. Try moving the dishwasher to a dedicated circuit.

faq.q: Is F47 expensive to repair?
faq.a: If it is just a loose connector, the fix is free. If a control board needs replacement, expect costs similar to F40 (200-600 total with installation).

title: Pressure Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is providing invalid readings.
fullDescription: Error F51 indicates the pressure sensor (or pressure switch) is giving readings outside the valid range. The control board cannot determine the water level in the sump.

causes.cause: Pressure sensor hose is blocked or disconnected
Pressure sensor has failed
Air trap chamber is clogged
Control board sensor input is faulty

symptoms: Error at random points during cycle

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the small rubber hose from the sump to the pressure sensor. Ensure it is connected, not kinked, and not blocked.

fixSteps.title: Clean air trap
fixSteps.instruction: The air trap where the hose connects to the sump can clog with detergent residue. Remove and clean it thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Blow gently into the sensor hose. Listen for clicks indicating the switch is working. No clicks means the sensor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the sensor from its mount, disconnect wiring and hose, and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If a new sensor and clean hose do not resolve F51, the control board input circuit may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F51 pressure sensor water level error

faq.q: How does the dishwasher measure water level?
faq.a: A pressure sensor connected to the sump via a hose measures the air pressure created by rising water. This tells the control board how much water is in the machine.

faq.q: Can a clogged air trap cause F51?
faq.a: Yes. Detergent residue can block the air trap, preventing the sensor from detecting water level changes accurately.

faq.q: Is F51 common?
faq.a: F51 is moderately common, especially in older machines or those in hard water areas. Regular maintenance helps prevent it.

title: Turbidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water turbidity sensor (soil sensor) is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error F52 indicates the turbidity sensor that measures how dirty the wash water is has failed. This sensor helps the dishwasher optimize water changes and cycle length based on soil level.

causes.cause: Turbidity sensor is coated with grease or film
Turbidity sensor has failed electronically
Wiring to sensor is damaged
Control board input fault

fixSteps.title: Clean the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the sensor in the sump area (usually a transparent window or lens). Clean it with a soft cloth and mild detergent. Grease buildup is the most common cause.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run an empty intensive cycle with Miele dishwasher cleaner to clear any residue from the sensor and system.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor connectivity
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sensor connector and check for secure connection. Clean any corrosion on the pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve F52, replace the turbidity sensor unit.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not resolve F52, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F52 turbidity soil sensor error fix

faq.q: What is a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: A turbidity sensor uses light to measure how cloudy or dirty the wash water is. Clear water means dishes are clean, cloudy water means more washing is needed.

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher without the turbidity sensor?
faq.a: The dishwasher may still run but cannot optimize cycle length or water usage. It may use default settings that are less efficient.

faq.q: How often should I clean the turbidity sensor?
faq.a: The sensor is usually self-cleaning during normal cycles. Running a monthly cleaning cycle with dishwasher cleaner helps keep it clear.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The circulation pump motor speed sensor is not providing valid readings.
fullDescription: Error F53 means the speed sensor on the circulation pump motor cannot detect rotation or is sending erratic signals. The control board uses this to regulate pump speed.

causes.cause: Speed sensor has failed
Pump motor is seized or blocked
Wiring to speed sensor is damaged
Inverter module not driving the motor

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. The sensor may recover from a temporary fault.

fixSteps.title: Check for pump blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the sump and pump area for debris that could prevent the pump from spinning.

fixSteps.title: Test the speed sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump motor and test the speed sensor resistance. Compare to service manual specifications.

fixSteps.title: Replace pump or sensor
fixSteps.instruction: On some models the speed sensor is replaceable separately. On others, the entire pump assembly must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: Pump replacement involves water connections and electrical work. Professional service is recommended for reliable results.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F53 circulation pump speed sensor error

faq.q: Is the speed sensor part of the pump?
faq.a: On many Miele models, the speed sensor is integrated into the circulation pump motor and cannot be replaced separately. The entire pump assembly is replaced.

faq.q: Can a blocked pump cause F53?
faq.a: Yes. If debris prevents the pump from spinning, the speed sensor reports no rotation, triggering F53. Always check for blockages first.

faq.q: How long does a Miele circulation pump last?
faq.a: Typically 8-15 years. Pumps in hard water areas may have shorter lifespans due to mineral buildup.

title: Circulation Pump Blocked
shortDescription: The circulation pump is mechanically blocked and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error F67 indicates the circulation pump cannot turn due to a mechanical blockage. A foreign object, broken glass, or heavy food debris is preventing the pump impeller from rotating.

causes.cause: Foreign object jamming the pump impeller
Pump bearings are seized
Heavy grease or food buildup in pump housing
Broken glass fragments in pump

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all filter assemblies and clean the sump area. Remove any visible debris, broken glass, or food particles.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller access
fixSteps.instruction: Some Miele dishwashers have an access cover over the circulation pump impeller in the sump. Remove it and check for obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Manually rotate the impeller
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, try to rotate the pump impeller by hand. It should spin freely. Remove any objects preventing rotation.

fixSteps.title: Access pump from below
fixSteps.instruction: If the blockage cannot be cleared from above, tilt the dishwasher and access the pump from underneath. Disconnect and clean the pump housing.

whenToCallPro: If the pump bearings are seized and the impeller cannot be freed, the pump needs replacement by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F67 circulation pump blocked error fix

faq.q: What commonly blocks the circulation pump?
faq.a: Broken glass, bones, fruit pits, toothpicks, and food labels are the most common items that jam the pump. Always scrape dishes and remove labels before loading.

faq.q: Can I access the pump without removing the dishwasher?
faq.a: On many Miele models, the pump impeller is accessible from inside the tub through the sump area. Remove filters and look for a pump access cover.

faq.q: Will running the pump while blocked damage it?
faq.a: Yes. A blocked pump motor can overheat and burn out. If you hear humming but no water circulation, turn off the dishwasher and clear the blockage.

title: Rinse Aid Dispenser Error
shortDescription: The rinse aid dispenser mechanism is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error F68 indicates the rinse aid dispenser is not opening or dosing correctly during the rinse phase. The solenoid or mechanism that releases rinse aid has failed.

causes.cause: Rinse aid dispenser solenoid has failed
Dispenser mechanism is stuck with residue
Wiring to dispenser is damaged
Dispenser assembly is cracked or broken

symptoms: Water spots on dishes and glasses
Rinse aid compartment still full after cycle
No rinse aid scent during rinse

fixSteps.title: Clean the dispenser
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rinse aid compartment and clean away any dried residue or buildup. Use warm water and a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check dispenser mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Manually try to operate the dispenser lid. It should open and close freely. Clear any stuck residue.

fixSteps.title: Test dispenser solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Access the back of the dispenser from the inner door panel. Test the solenoid coil resistance (typical values vary). No continuity means the solenoid has failed.

fixSteps.title: Replace dispenser assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the solenoid or mechanism is faulty, replace the entire dispenser assembly. Remove inner door panel screws and swap the unit.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and solenoid replacement do not fix F68, the control board may not be sending the signal to the dispenser.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F68 rinse aid dispenser error fix

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher without rinse aid?
faq.a: Yes, but dishes may have water spots and glasses may be cloudy. Rinse aid helps water sheet off surfaces for spot-free drying.

faq.q: Why is my rinse aid compartment still full?
faq.a: The dispenser mechanism may be stuck or the solenoid has failed. Clean the dispenser area first, then test the solenoid if cleaning does not help.

faq.q: Can I use alternative rinse aids?
faq.a: Miele recommends their own rinse aid for best results, but most standard dishwasher rinse aids are compatible.

title: Detergent Dispenser Error
shortDescription: The detergent dispenser is not opening at the correct time during the cycle.
fullDescription: Error F69 means the detergent dispenser is not releasing detergent at the programmed point in the wash cycle. The dispenser lid mechanism, solenoid, or feedback sensor has failed.

causes.cause: Dispenser solenoid has failed
Dispenser lid is stuck with dried detergent
Dispenser spring or hinge is broken
Wiring to dispenser is faulty

symptoms: Detergent tablet still in dispenser after cycle
Dishes not clean due to no detergent release
Dispenser lid does not pop open during wash

fixSteps.title: Clean dispenser thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dispenser and scrub away all dried detergent. Use hot water and a brush to clean the lid, hinge, and compartment.

fixSteps.title: Test manual operation
fixSteps.instruction: Close the dispenser lid and press the release latch. The lid should spring open firmly. If it sticks, clean the mechanism further or check the spring.

fixSteps.title: Test the solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Access the dispenser from inside the door panel. Test the release solenoid for proper resistance. If open circuit, replace the dispenser assembly.

fixSteps.title: Replace dispenser
fixSteps.instruction: If the mechanism is broken, replace the entire detergent dispenser assembly from the inner door.

whenToCallPro: If the dispenser mechanism is intact but the solenoid does not receive power, the control board needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F69 detergent dispenser not opening error

faq.q: Why is my detergent tablet still in the dispenser?
faq.a: The dispenser lid did not open during the cycle. This could be due to dried detergent preventing the lid from opening, a broken spring, or a failed solenoid.

faq.q: Can I put the detergent directly in the tub?
faq.a: As a temporary workaround, you can place a detergent tablet on the bottom of the tub. However, this bypasses the timed release and may reduce wash effectiveness.

faq.q: Should I use liquid or powder detergent?
faq.a: Miele recommends their own PowerDisk or tablets. Liquid and powder detergents work but ensure the dispenser is clean and dry before adding powder to prevent caking.

title: Waterproof System Activated
shortDescription: The built-in leak protection system has detected water in the base of the machine.
fullDescription: Error F70 is a critical safety alert. The float switch in the dishwasher's base pan has detected standing water, indicating a leak from an internal component. The machine shuts down immediately and activates the drain pump to minimize water damage.

causes.cause: Internal hose connection is leaking
Door seal is worn or damaged
Circulation pump gasket is leaking
Sump or tub has a crack
Float switch is stuck in activated position

symptoms: Water on the floor under or around dishwasher
Water visible when tilting the machine

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately close the water supply valve for the dishwasher. Unplug the machine. Place towels around the base.

fixSteps.title: Check the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the dishwasher back to access the base. Check for standing water and dry it with towels or a sponge. Free the float switch if stuck.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Look for water trails on internal components. Common leak points: door seal, sump area hoses, circulation pump connections, and inlet valve.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the leaking gasket, hose clamp, or seal. Ensure all connections are tight and leak-free before restoring power and water.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source cannot be identified, or if the sump/tub is cracked, professional service is essential to prevent recurring water damage.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F70 waterproof leak protection error fix

faq.q: Is F70 an emergency?
faq.a: F70 means water is leaking inside the machine. Turn off the water supply immediately. While the Waterproof System limits damage, the leak should be repaired before using the dishwasher again.

faq.q: Why does the drain pump run non-stop with F70?
faq.a: The Waterproof System activates the drain pump to remove as much water as possible from the machine when a leak is detected. This minimizes potential water damage.

faq.q: Can a simple reset fix F70?
faq.a: You can clear F70 temporarily by drying the base pan and freeing the float switch. However, unless the leak is repaired, F70 will return.

title: Circulation Pump Motor Error
shortDescription: The circulation pump motor is not running at the expected speed or has stalled.
fullDescription: Error F78 specifically targets the circulation pump motor performance. The motor is not achieving or maintaining the commanded speed, potentially due to electrical or mechanical issues with the pump.

causes.cause: Pump motor winding failure
Pump impeller is partially blocked
Motor capacitor has failed (on AC models)
Motor overheating due to poor ventilation

fixSteps.title: Check for pump blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the sump and check the pump area for debris. A partial blockage can overload the motor.

fixSteps.title: Let motor cool
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump ran excessively, let it cool for 30 minutes. Overheating can cause temporary performance loss.

fixSteps.title: Test pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test the motor winding resistance. Compare to specifications. Also check for insulation breakdown.

fixSteps.title: Replace circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor tests faulty, replace the entire circulation pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: Pump motor replacement involves removing the pump from underneath the dishwasher and reconnecting water and electrical connections. Professional service is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F78 circulation pump motor error

faq.q: How do I know if my circulation pump is failing?
faq.a: Signs include poor wash results, unusual noises during wash, the pump running but not producing strong water spray, and frequent error codes related to water circulation.

faq.q: Is a Miele circulation pump expensive?
faq.a: Genuine Miele circulation pump assemblies cost 150-300 dollars. Professional installation adds 100-200 dollars. This is a significant but worthwhile repair for a quality dishwasher.

faq.q: Can I extend my pump's life?
faq.a: Regular filter cleaning, running monthly cleaning cycles, and using quality detergent helps prevent premature pump wear. Avoid letting food debris enter the sump.

title: Spray Arm Position Sensor Error
shortDescription: The spray arm position or rotation sensor is not detecting proper arm movement.
fullDescription: Error F84 means the sensor that detects spray arm rotation has flagged an issue. The spray arm may not be spinning, or the sensor itself has failed. Proper spray arm rotation is essential for even dish cleaning.

causes.cause: Spray arm is blocked by dishes or utensils
Spray arm bearing is worn and not rotating freely
Rotation sensor has failed
Spray arm nozzles are clogged reducing rotation force

symptoms: Clicking sound as arm hits an obstruction

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher and manually spin each spray arm. Ensure no tall items, utensils, or dishes are blocking rotation. Rearrange the load.

fixSteps.title: Clean spray arm nozzles
fixSteps.instruction: Remove spray arms and clear each nozzle with a toothpick. Clogged nozzles reduce the jet force needed to spin the arm.

fixSteps.title: Check spray arm bearings
fixSteps.instruction: With arms removed, check the bearing hub for wear. The arm should spin freely with no wobble. Replace if worn.

fixSteps.title: Test rotation sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If spray arms spin freely but F84 persists, the rotation sensor in the sump area may be faulty. Test or replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor needs replacement and cannot be accessed easily, or if the error persists after all checks, contact a technician.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F84 spray arm rotation sensor error

faq.q: Why are my spray arms not spinning?
faq.a: The most common reason is dishes or utensils blocking the arm path. Always ensure tall items do not extend into the spray arm rotation zone.

faq.q: How do I know if spray arms are working?
faq.a: After starting a cycle, open the door after a few minutes (carefully, hot water) and check if spray arm positions have changed. You can also listen for the swooshing sound of water.

faq.q: Can clogged nozzles stop the spray arm from spinning?
faq.a: Yes. The jet force from the nozzles drives the arm rotation. If too many nozzles are blocked, the arm cannot generate enough torque to spin.

title: Salt Container Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water softener salt level sensor or regeneration system has a fault.
fullDescription: Error F85 relates to the built-in water softener system. The salt level sensor is giving invalid readings or the regeneration valve is not functioning correctly. Miele dishwashers with built-in water softeners use salt to regenerate the ion exchange resin.

causes.cause: Salt container is empty
Salt sensor is caked with deposits
Regeneration valve is stuck
Salt sensor has failed electronically

symptoms: F85 error or salt indicator on display
Hard water spots on dishes and glasses

fixSteps.title: Refill salt
fixSteps.instruction: Open the salt compartment cap on the floor of the dishwasher. Fill with dishwasher salt (not table salt). Ensure the cap is securely closed.

fixSteps.title: Clean the salt sensor
fixSteps.instruction: With the salt compartment open, clean around the sensor area. Remove any caked salt deposits that may be interfering with the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check water softener settings
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water hardness setting matches your local water supply. Incorrect settings can cause regeneration issues.

fixSteps.title: Test or replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If refilling and cleaning do not clear F85, the salt level sensor or regeneration valve may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the regeneration valve or sensor needs replacement, professional service ensures the water softener system is properly calibrated.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dishwasher F85 salt sensor softener error fix

faq.q: What salt should I use in my Miele dishwasher?
faq.a: Use only coarse-grained dishwasher salt (regeneration salt). Never use table salt, rock salt, or other types as they contain additives that can damage the softener system.

faq.q: How often do I need to add salt?
faq.a: This depends on water hardness and usage. The dishwasher will indicate when salt is low. In hard water areas, you may need to refill every 1-2 months.

faq.q: Do I need salt if I use all-in-one tablets?
faq.a: Miele recommends still using salt and rinse aid even with all-in-one tablets, especially in hard water areas. The built-in softener is more effective than the small amount of softener in tablets.
